entry.title: Creating an MCP Server in SAP Integration Suite (Trial) – A Hands-on Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Integration Suite now provides an option to create and expose an MCP Server from within the Integration Suite environment. I recently tested this feature in an SAP BTP trial account and wanted to share a short, practical walkthrough based on my hands-on experience.
The goal of this blog is simple: show how to create, publish, and test an MCP Server using SAP Integration Suite in a trial setup.

More details about available Integration Suite service plans and capabilities can be found in SAP Note 2903776 - SAP Integration Suite – Service Plans and Upgrade Paths

Prerequisites

Before starting, make sure you have:

  • An active SAP BTP trial account
  • Access to SAP Integration Suite 
  • Required capabilities activated, especially:
    • Integration Cell
  • A test API or service that you want to expose
  • Basic understanding of APIs and SAP BTP cockpit navigation

Step 1: Enable the Required Capability

  1. Inside Integration Suite, go to Settings → Runtimes
  2. Check if the Integration Cell runtime is available (if not, please wait until your tenant has it)
  3. Enable the feature ~ takes a while but works (if not already active)

Step 2: Create MCP Server Configuration

Go to an existing or new Integration Package.
Create a new artifact of type: MCP Server

Then maintain the basic configuration:

  • Select the source type
    • In this example: API
  • Provide the required details
  • Ensure that the MCP path is unique
  • Select the required tools based on the API resources
  • Save the artifact

After the artifact is created:

  • Open the MCP configuration
  • Add the required resources
  • Save it as a version and Deploy the MCP Server
  • Select the runtime and virtual host
Once deployment is successful, the MCP Server is active.
 
 

 

Step 3: Publish MCP Server in Developer Hub

After deployment, the MCP Server needs to be published so that it can be discovered and consumed.

Go to Developer Hub and add new content.
Then:

  • Select the relevant business system
  • Choose the Integration Suite business system
  • Go to MCP Servers
  • Select the MCP Server you want to publish
  • Create a product
  • Publish the product
  • Subscribe to the product to get the required credentials

This step is important because the client tool needs connection details and credentials to access the MCP Server.


Step 4: Testing the MCP Server

For testing, I used Cline as the AI tool.
The high-level steps were:

  • Add the newly created MCP Server to the AI tool
  • Maintain the server URL and credentials (basic auth - enabled in base API)
  • Make sure the server is active and allowed
  • Ask a question that should trigger the tool call
  • Validate the response from the MCP Server

In my test, the AI tool was able to call the MCP Server and receive a response successfully.

Personal Experience

Overall, the setup was quite straightforward once the required runtime was available.

What worked well

  • MCP Server artifact creation is integrated into the familiar Integration Suite package model.
  • Publishing through Developer Hub makes the server easier to consume.
  • Tool selection based on API resources is simple to understand.
  • Testing from an AI tool like Cline gives quick validation.

Key observations

  • The MCP path must be unique, so naming needs attention.
  • Credentials and server configuration in the AI client must be maintained carefully.
  • For first testing, it is better to start with a simple API with basic auth enabled.

Conclusion

The MCP Server feature in SAP Integration Suite is a useful step toward exposing enterprise APIs for AI-based consumption in a controlled way.

From my hands-on trial account experience, the setup flow is understandable and can be tested end-to-end with a simple API and an MCP-compatible client.

For anyone exploring AI integration scenarios with SAP Integration Suite, this is a good feature to try in a trial environment first

References

  • 2903776 - SAP Integration Suite – Service Plans and Upgrade Paths
  • API-Centric Integration | SAP Help Portal
  • Model Context Protocol (MCP) | SAP Help Portal
  • Create an MCP Server from an API Artifact | SAP Help Portal
  • Limits in API-Centric Integration | SAP Help Portal
